Title: Gene clustering and copy number variation in alkaloid metabolic pathways of opium poppy

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: PS7; M; PS1; PS4; BC; L; P; T; 11; 40
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical benzylisoquinoline alkaloid with medical/industrial importance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Extreme expression in latex; covaries positively with noscapine production
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental time course and tissue comparison under controlled growth conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Hi-C; RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA508405); whole-genome resequencing (BioProject PRJNA508405); metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher copy number/expression is associated with high noscapine production; massively duplicated in several high-noscapine cultivars
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome resequencing-based CNV analysis; RNA-seq coexpression; metabolite covariation analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for boosting noscapine production in breeding or synthetic biology; prioritize alongside noscapine-cluster genes for functional testing
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function remains unknown; identified as a strong candidate based on clustering, latex expression, CNV, and metabolite covariation rather than direct biochemical assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MLP15 is a highly latex-expressed PR10/MLP-family gene of unknown biochemical function. 2. It is massively duplicated in several cultivars with high noscapine accumulation. 3. Its expression positively covaries with noscapine production, making it a strong candidate contributor to alkaloid pathway functionality.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical opiate alkaloid for pain relief
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Tightly co-expressed with key morphine/thebaine genes in latex
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Latex-enriched; strongly covaries with morphine pathway end products
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental time course and tissue comparison
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is strongly associated with morphine-pathway alkaloid accumulation, suggesting a positive role in pathway flux or functionality
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq coexpression and gene–metabolite covariation analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate for pathway completion and engineering of morphinan production; requires direct functional testing
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unknown function; causal role inferred from coordinated expression and metabolite covariance, not directly demonstrated
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MLP2 is tightly co-expressed with latex-expressed morphine/thebaine genes. 2. It strongly covaries with morphine pathway end products. 3. It is prioritized as a candidate non-enzymatic component needed for in planta alkaloid production.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical opiate precursor used industrially for semisynthetic opioids
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated across tissues/timepoints with thebaine accumulation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental time course and tissue comparison
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is strongly associated with thebaine accumulation, suggesting involvement in thebaine pathway functionality
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate accessory gene for improving thebaine biosynthesis in plant or microbial engineering after functional validation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MLP1 is one of the unknown-function genes most strongly associated with thebaine levels. 2. Its transcript abundance covaries with thebaine across tissues and developmental stages. 3. It represents a candidate accessory component of opiate biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmaceutical opiate alkaloid for pain relief
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex; tightly co-expressed with morphine/thebaine genes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong positive covariation with morphine pathway end products
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental time course and tissue comparison
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA508405); metabolite profiling; genome assembly/clustering
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression pattern implicates PR10-5 as a positive candidate contributor to morphine-pathway alkaloid accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cluster localization; RNA-seq coexpression; gene–metabolite covariation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate accessory factor for increasing morphinan output; suitable for future functional validation and pathway engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expanded by tandem duplication with flanking TE duplications; function unknown
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR10-5 resides in a clustered, tandemly duplicated region. 2. It is highly expressed in latex and tightly co-expressed with morphine/thebaine genes. 3. Its expression strongly tracks morphine pathway end products, supporting candidacy as a functional alkaloid-pathway component.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with thebaine accumulation across tissues/timepoints
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental time course and tissue comparison
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is strongly associated with thebaine accumulation, indicating likely positive contribution to pathway functionality
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for improving thebaine production after direct functional validation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR10-14 is among the strongest unknown-function correlates of thebaine accumulation. 2. Its transcript pattern mirrors thebaine levels across sampled tissues and stages. 3. It is a priority candidate for future pathway-completion studies.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Morphine and codeine are pharmaceutical opiates; thebaine is an industrial opiate precursor
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for conversion of thebaine toward downstream morphinan products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: 2-oxoglutarate/Fe(II)-dependent oxidative O-demethylation of thebaine in the morphinan pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a critical control point by CNV deletion phenotype, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in latex; genes including and downstream of T6ODM are mainly latex expressed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage and tissue context; cultivar-specific CNV
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq (PRJNA508405); whole-genome resequencing (PRJNA508405); metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of the tandemly duplicated T6ODM cluster is associated with loss of morphine and codeine production and strong accumulation of thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cultivar CNV analysis linked to alkaloid profiling; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T6ODM occurs as a tandemly duplicated cluster in opium poppy. 2. Its expression is associated with latex-localized late morphinan biosynthesis. 3. Deletion of the T6ODM cluster in cultivar T correlates with absent morphine/codeine and elevated thebaine, demonstrating a decisive pathway role.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Multiple paralogs clustered with duplicated flanking transposable elements
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion shifts flux from morphine/codeine toward thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating T6ODM copy number is a practical route to tune morphine/codeine versus thebaine output in breeding or engineering

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of thebaine from salutaridinol-7-O-acetate-related intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Part of the baine paralog cluster; associated with thebaine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Assigned as the enzyme producing thebaine and mapped within a clustered thebaine-related genomic region
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior functional characterization cited and genome localization in this study
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THS is positioned in a previously unmapped baine-gene cluster with SalR and SalAT. 2. Its localization supports the strong clustering of thebaine-pathway genes. 3. The study treats THS as a key known enzyme in the thebaine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper does not newly biochemically validate THS, but uses its genomic placement to analyze clustering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important enzyme target for thebaine-oriented synthetic biology and pathway reconstruction

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: PS7; L; P; T; 11; 40 and others
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal benzylisoquinoline alkaloid with pharmaceutical importance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex; stem-derived tissues analyzed for cultivar comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: NISO copies show expression patterns differing from other thebaine clusters; also described among previously unanchored important BIA genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/clustering; RNA-seq; resequencing CNV; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the noscapine pathway gene set whose clustered organization and copy variation relate to noscapine production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome localization, coexpression context, cultivar CNV correlated with noscapine phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NISO was among previously unanchored key BIA genes placed onto chromosome-scale scaffolds by the improved assembly. 2. It occurs in tandemly duplicated arrays with duplicated flanking TEs. 3. As a noscapine-pathway gene, its pathway deletion in multiple cultivars is linked to loss of noscapine production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper emphasizes genomic architecture rather than direct enzymology for NISO
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion contributes to absence of noscapine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker within the noscapine gene cluster for selecting or engineering noscapine-producing lines

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Copy number dramatically increases in some cultivars, implying potential contribution to increased pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7OMT shows dramatic copy-number increase in some cultivars. 2. This suggests that duplication of pathway enzymes may boost alkaloid flux. 3. The study highlights 7OMT as part of the dynamic evolution of BIA biosynthetic genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not map a specific end-product consequence for 7OMT CNV in the same detail as for T6ODM or noscapine-cluster deletion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential dosage-based engineering target for elevating BIA flux

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: PS7; L; P; T; 11; 40 and others
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex; stem tissues used for cultivar metabolite comparison
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms or is required for final noscapine production
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Noscapine or immediate precursor/product in noscapine branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern in noscapine cluster differs from CXE2 and is not well correlated despite clustering
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cultivar-specific deletion of entire noscapine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; resequencing CNV; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of the 11-gene noscapine pathway including NOS is linked to lack of noscapine production in five cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cultivar CNV linked to metabolite phenotype; gene-expression analysis within cluster
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NOS lies within the highly clustered noscapine pathway region. 2. The entire noscapine cluster is deleted in five cultivars lacking noscapine. 3. Despite cluster membership, NOS expression is not strongly correlated with CXE2, highlighting nonuniform coexpression within clusters.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes cluster behavior and deletion phenotype more than direct mechanistic characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key marker gene for selecting noscapine-producing germplasm or reconstructing the noscapine pathway
